trailingComments.value:
*
 * Analog Clock Colors Options
 * @typedef {Object} AnalogClockColors
 * @property {String} [number="#000000"] Number color
 * @property {String} [hourHand="#000000"] Hour hand color
 * @property {String} [minuteHand="#000000"] Minute hand color
 * @property {String} [secondHand="#000000"] Second hand color

*
 * Analog Clock Widget Options
 * @typedef {Object} AnalogClockOptions
 * @property {String} [fontFamily="Monospace"] Font family
 * @property {AnalogClockColors} [colors] Clock colors
 * @property {String[]} [labels] Color labels

leadingComments.value: * The default starting size for this widget.

trailingComments.value: * The minimum dimension for this widget.

leadingComments.value: * The minimum dimension for this widget.

*
	 * Creates a new instance.
	 * @param {Core} core MeeseOS Core instance reference
	 * @param {AnalogClockOptions} [options] Instance options
Always work from the canvas's current pixel size. The base render loop
captures the starting dimensions once, so reading them here keeps the
clock centered and circular after the widget is resized to any aspect
ratio (fixes off-center numbers on tall/wide widgets).

trailingComments.value: Reset the transform every frame and clear, rather than translating once
and relying on the matrix persisting (it is reset whenever the canvas
is resized, which is exactly when the old approach broke).

leadingComments.value: Reset the transform every frame and clear, rather than translating once
and relying on the matrix persisting (it is reset whenever the canvas
is resized, which is exactly when the old approach broke).

*
 * Analog Clock Colors Options
 * @typedef {Object} AnalogClockColors
 * @property {String} [number="#000000"] Number color
 * @property {String} [hourHand="#000000"] Hour hand color
 * @property {String} [minuteHand="#000000"] Minute hand color
 * @property {String} [secondHand="#000000"] Second hand color

*
 * Analog Clock Widget Options
 * @typedef {Object} AnalogClockOptions
 * @property {String} [fontFamily="Monospace"] Font family
 * @property {AnalogClockColors} [colors] Clock colors
 * @property {String[]} [labels] Color labels

*
	 * Creates a new instance.
	 * @param {Core} core MeeseOS Core instance reference
	 * @param {AnalogClockOptions} [options] Instance options
* The default starting size for this widget.
* The minimum dimension for this widget.
Always work from the canvas's current pixel size. The base render loop
captures the starting dimensions once, so reading them here keeps the
clock centered and circular after the widget is resized to any aspect
ratio (fixes off-center numbers on tall/wide widgets).
Reset the transform every frame and clear, rather than translating once
and relying on the matrix persisting (it is reset whenever the canvas
is resized, which is exactly when the old approach broke).

*
	 * Creates a color dialog for all of the widget's properties.

	 * @private

	 * @param {Number} radius the radius of the clock
	 * @private

*

 * @typedef {Object} AnalogClockColors

 * @property {String} [hourHand="#000000"] Hour hand color
 * @property {String} [minuteHand="#000000"] Minute hand color
 * @property {String} [secondHand="#000000"] Second hand color

*

 * @property {String} [fontFamily="Monospace"] Font family

* The default starting size for this widget.
* The minimum dimension for this widget.
Always work from the canvas's current pixel size. The base render loop
captures the starting dimensions once, so reading them here keeps the
clock centered and circular after the widget is resized to any aspect
ratio (fixes off-center numbers on tall/wide widgets).
Reset the transform every frame and clear, rather than translating once
and relying on the matrix persisting (it is reset whenever the canvas
is resized, which is exactly when the old approach broke).
